This advisory has been withdrawn because it is a duplicate of GHSA-r6xh-pqhr-v4xh. This link is maintained to preserve external references.

### Original Description
OpenClaw before 2026.4.22 derives loopback MCP owner context from spoofable server-issued bearer tokens in request headers. Non-owner loopback clients can present themselves as owner to bypass owner-gated operations by manipulating the sender-owner header metadata.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-35vf-vw9f-q3cr", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-11T16:12:46Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-35vf-vw9f-q3cr", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-35vf-vw9f-q3cr"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-06T20:16:35Z", "published_at": "2026-05-06T21:31:42Z", "references": ["https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-r6xh-pqhr-v4xh", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44118", "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3cb1a56bfc9579a0f2336f9cfa12a8a744332a19", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/openclaw-owner-context-spoofing-via-bearer-token-header", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-35vf-vw9f-q3cr"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "", "summary": "Duplicate Advisory: OpenClaw: MCP loopback owner context is derived from server-issued bearer tokens", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-11T16:12:48Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-35vf-vw9f-q3cr", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2026.4.22", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "openclaw"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2026.4.22"}], "withdrawn_at": "2026-05-11T16:12:46Z"}
